content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with all atom indices labeled. Let me examine atom C:49 (carbon, index 49) more carefully to identify all its neighbor atoms.

From the image, I can see atom C:49 in the center-right portion of the molecule. Looking at its connections:

- C:49 is connected to N:44 (nitrogen) - visible as a blue sphere directly bonded to C:49
- C:49 is connected to C:41 (carbon) - visible as a yellow/gold sphere to the left of C:49
- C:49 is connected to C:47 (carbon) - visible as a yellow/gold sphere below C:49
- C:49 is connected to H:58 (hydrogen) - visible as a white sphere above C:49

Let me verify these connections by examining the bonds more closely. In the image, I can clearly see four bonds emanating from C:49:
1. To N:44 (blue sphere)
2. To C:41 (yellow/gold sphere)
3. To C:47 (yellow/gold sphere)
4. To H:58 (white sphere)

The geomconf_index for this molecule is 6203, as indicated by the filename "geomconf_6203.xyz".

Therefore, the neighbor atoms of atom C:49 are:
- Atom index 44, element N (nitrogen)
- Atom index 41, element C (carbon)
- Atom index 47, element C (carbon)
- Atom index 58, element H (hydrogen)

Geomconf_index: 6203

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 49, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
